newFeatures.title: Universal Ranking Modal
newFeatures.description: Completely redesigned ranking system for movies and TV shows with watch history matrix, date range tracking, and progress indicators.
details: Watch history matrix with SINGLE DATE, DATE RANGE, and LONG AGO options
Visual progress indicators showing watch completion percentage
Currently watching and DNF (Did Not Finish) status options
Rank override functionality for reclassifying entries
Integrated watchlist management within the modal

newFeatures.title: Enhanced List Rankings
newFeatures.description: Improved drag-and-drop functionality and cleaner interface design.
details: Drag and drop between different class sections
Removed movement arrows for cleaner interface
Larger portraits for creators, directors, and actors
Moved EDIT WATCHES button for better accessibility
Added filtering capabilities back to lists

newFeatures.title: Profile Viewer Enhancements
newFeatures.description: Expanded profile pages with more comprehensive ranking views.
details: Scroll through ALL of a user's ranked items
Quick access RANK and ADD UNRANKED buttons for easy saving
Top 10 actors and directors display
Better navigation for discovering new content from friends

newFeatures.title: Advanced Search Features
newFeatures.description: Powerful new search options with depth control and better results.
details: Query toggles for Movies/TV Shows/People filtering
Search depth options: SIMPLE (basic info) or EXTENSIVE (full details)
Browse tab for discovering top-ranked content
Cross-referenced search between actors/directors and their works

futureFeatures.title: Movie Clubs
futureFeatures.description: Social groups for sharing and discussing movies with friends.
details: Create and join movie clubs with friends
View member watchlists and overlaps
Track recent watches and current progress
Send movie suggestions (similar or blind suggestions)
Comment system for friendly trash talk and discussions

futureFeatures.title: Inbox System
futureFeatures.description: Central hub for updates from movie clubs and official Clastone communications.
details: Receive movie club updates and invitations
Get official patch notes and announcements
Notification management system

futureFeatures.title: Enhanced Statistics
futureFeatures.description: Comprehensive analytics and insights about your viewing habits.
details: Detailed viewing statistics and trends
Genre preferences and patterns
Year-by-year breakdown analysis
Comparison statistics with friends

futureFeatures.title: Database Optimizations
futureFeatures.description: Performance improvements and reduced data usage.
details: Reduced total database reads and writes
Optimized batch processing with longer waits
Streamlined data structures for better performance

newFeatures.title: Class-Based Ranking System
newFeatures.description: Revolutionary ranking system using classes instead of numerical scores.
details: S (OLYMPUS), A (DAMN_GOOD), B (GOOD), C (ALRIGHT), D (MEH), F (BAD) classes
Special classes: BABY, UNRANKED, DELICIOUS_GARBAGE
Percentile-based rankings within classes
Manual ordering within each class

newFeatures.title: Multi-Type Media Tracking
newFeatures.description: Comprehensive tracking for movies, TV shows, actors, and directors.
details: Separate lists for Movies, TV Shows, Actors, Directors
Watchlist functionality for all media types
Detailed watch history with date tracking
Progress tracking for TV shows

newFeatures.title: Social Features
newFeatures.description: Connect with friends and share your rankings.
details: Friends system with profile viewing
Compare rankings and discover new content
Top 10 lists display
Recently watched tracking

content.content:
Clastone is a social tool for being able to accurately rank and view your previous/current/future watches of everything you have ever seen in a class-based superlist.

I think a class system is more accurate than giving a number score to a given entry, as when you have seen 500+ movies it can be hard to get a grasp on what a good score to give it is. So this way, you can group together movies which are similarly liked, and within each class have a rough ranking of which is better or worse than anything else. The most important ranking orders are always at the top and the bottom.

Personally, I'd start by going through and adding as many items you can to unranked classes, and then starting to add in your "baseline" movies and shows. That is, your top and bottom ranked ones, and what you feel is a good indicator of each of the middle classes. This is bound to change but you need to just start laying an even layer over all classes so you can truly understand what it means for a given entry to belong to its given class.

Main Navigation:
- Left Menu Items: Movies | TV Shows | Actors | Directors | Watchlist
- Right Menu Items: Search | Friends | Profile | Settings | Diagnostics
- Version Flag: Click the version flag next to logo to access patch notes

Mobile Navigation:
- Use the hamburger menu (☰) on mobile devices
- All navigation options available in mobile overlay
- Swipe or tap outside to close the mobile menu

I'm poor so we are having to use batch saving which means if you make a bunch of changes, wait at least 10s before reloading the site otherwise they might not save, I intend to add an indicator for this.

The system uses Firebase for data storage and TMDb for movie/TV metadata. Local storage is used as backup for offline access.
